TYPICAL TIMELINE:
Days 1-3: Initial access & enumeration
Days 4-7: Exploitation & lateral movement
Days 8-14: Privilege escalation & persistence
Days 15+: Objective completion & covering tracks

KEY DETECTION POINTS:
Initial Access: Unusual logons, failed auth attempts
Enumeration: LDAP queries, BloodHound signatures
Exploitation: Event 4769, certificate requests
Lateral Movement: Event 4624, unusual service creation
Persistence: ACL modifications, krbtgt access

DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH:
Network: Segmentation, SMB signing, LDAP signing
Identity: MFA everywhere, LAPS deployment
Endpoint: EDR deployment, LSA protection
Monitoring: Event log aggregation, anomaly detection
Hardening: Disable LLMNR/NBT-NS, audit ACLs regularly
